name: The Sanguire of Sandrun
motto: Fire is the only truth that does not lie about itself.
goal: Hold Sandrun as the indisputable seat of fire-bloodline strength on Tunya, and remind the kingdoms — without saying so plainly — that nothing the rest of the world builds survives a Sanguire refusal.
fears: the bloodline diluting through forced-hand intermarriages
Medici ice ever finding a counter to their fire
losing the Cree's quiet alliance
tension_hook: Sanguire are a Kree people, but they will not say which Kree people they were before Tunya. The other Kree subgroups know. They will not say either.

faction_state.tensions: Iyatte's youngest son was born without flame. He is hidden. The clans whisper that the dilution has reached the warlord's own table.

name: Kree
motto: Tunya was never ours. Kree always was.
goal: Preserve Kree as a single nation on Tunya, even as its peoples scatter (Sanguire to Sandrun, Medici among them, Cree on the coast). The Kree state is the constant; the peoples drift.
values: tolerance only for those who acknowledge the flag
fears: Sanguire forgetting they are Kree first
Medici defecting to a kingdom that learns their secret
Cree drifting fully independent
tension_hook: Kree is the only people on Tunya whose origin is not Earth and not Ancient Tunyan. They know what they are. They will not tell anyone, including their own younger generations.

faction_state.tensions: The Medici asked, last winter, if they could leave. The chancellor said yes. The chancellor lied.

name: The Medici
motto: We did not arrive. We were already on the way.
goal: Survive a fifth generation. Continue healing those who reach them. Eventually, choose whether to reveal what they are.
fears: the Kree formally claiming them
Sanguire learning the counter to fire
their own children losing the language they came with
tension_hook: The Medici are not from Earth. The Medici are not Ancient Tunyan. They live among Kree because Kree is the only people on Tunya who would not ask why.

faction_state.tensions: A Sanguire-Medici hybrid was born last Cull. The mother fled. The father is Sanguire. The child is somewhere. The Medici know where. They will not say.

name: Cree
motto: We walked off the ark. We did not arrive.
goal: Maintain coastal independence near Kree without joining it. Preserve the oral memory of what was lost on the North American ark before the curators got to it.
values: small-scale alliance with Sanguire (Sanguire never tried to claim them)
fears: Asbir asking what their ancestors deleted
Kree formal annexation
Fluxom's sea-trade returning
tension_hook: Cree's elders remember what Asbir's curators deleted. They are not asked, and so they do not say. But they remember.

faction_state.tensions: An Asbir scholar arrived last Vernal asking about the deleted archive. They were fed and turned around. The elders agreed: not yet.

name: Aekon
motto: We arrived last and built best.
goal: Hold the 12 districts as the most-prosperous, most-fertile civilization on Tunya. Pretend the stranded-transit decades did not change them. Keep the Bloc closed.
fears: the rest of Tunya learning what kept them sane in the wormhole
Bloc partners (Dinye, Asbir) breaking the silence
anything that reminds them of the years stranded
tension_hook: Aekon arrived with everyone alive. Not everyone arrived sane. The sky temple's twelfth district is closed for reasons no one in office today is alive to remember.

faction_state.tensions: The Bloc holds because Aekon needs it. Aekon needs it because the alternative is admitting what happened in transit.

name: Asbir
motto: We brought what was best. The rest is gone for a reason.
goal: Govern Asbir without the half of cultural memory the curators erased. Convince themselves that what was deleted was lesser. Shut down anyone trying to undelete it.
fears: scholars finding the deletion logs
Cree elders speaking up
the Asbir ark archive booting cleanly enough to show what's missing
tension_hook: What was scrubbed before launch is recoverable from the Asbir ark with enough solar charge. The Lord Curator knows. He has been the only one allowed near the terminal for thirty years.

faction_state.tensions: A junior scholar found the index of deletions, not the deletions themselves, but the index. He has not yet decided who to tell.

name: Sahm
motto: We arrived as a people. We have not been absorbed.
goal: Hold Sahm as a self-determining nation distinct from Sandrun, despite shared Earth-origin. Out-build, out-think, out-last every neighbor that mistakes their dignity for something they need to earn.
fears: being treated as a subset of Sandrun
the deletion of Sahm history once a generation forgets it
Fluxom's sea-trade returning with the old labels
tension_hook: Sahm's smiths can match Sanguire on iron. The Sanguire know. Neither side speaks of it openly because the day they do is the day the contest begins.

faction_state.tensions: Dormas's textile lords offered a Sahm scholar a chair on condition of changing how she taught the arrival. She refused. They are not done asking.

name: Dinye
motto: The land speaks first. We answer second. The cities never get a turn.
goal: Maintain the seven elder-led villages without yielding to a city's gravity. Remain in the Bloc as a balancing voice. Never tell anyone what they remember from before the arrival.
fears: a city forming inside Dinye's borders
the elders dying without successors who can hear the land
the Bloc breaking under Aekon's strain
tension_hook: Dinye's elders are the only Bloc voice that knows why the Bloc holds — they listened to Aekon's land before Aekon could speak.

faction_state.tensions: A young Dinye walker keeps trying to map the villages onto a city plan. The elders have not stopped him. They are watching what he becomes.

name: Bahiij
goal: Maintain the social contract that keeps Bahiij joyful in public. Internally, manage the cost of that contract before it cracks.
fears: a public crack in the radiance
elephants sickening
the kingdom recognizing how much of its joy is performed
tension_hook: Bahiij joy is a four-generation-old contract: the people who arrived second-to-last must be radiant or vanish. The festival master's quiet quarter is where the cost is paid.

faction_state.tensions: The quiet quarter has grown. The raja knows. The raja is from the quiet quarter.

name: Masond
motto: We were here. We built every wall that holds.
goal: Hold Masond as the rooted Tunyan kingdom — bricklayers, builders, the bones of every other city's first quarter. Carry forward the half of Ancient Tunyan memory the arrivals do not have.
fears: Nil refusing to share the other half of the memory
an arrival kingdom claiming a Masond foundation as their own
the bricks being torn up faster than they can be laid
tension_hook: Masond knows half of what Nil knows. Nil refuses to share the rest. Masond does not push. Masond waits four generations more if it must.

faction_state.tensions: A young Masond architect lived in Nil for one season. He returned with five new mason marks and would not say what they meant.

name: Dormas
motto: Our servants would be your nobles.
goal: Maintain the textile economy and the inverted social hierarchy. Keep the upper class invisible enough that no one asks what makes them eligible for it.
fears: the question 'who is your upper class, exactly'
the textile trade collapsing
a Dormas servant defecting and writing a memoir
tension_hook: Dormas's servants are the rest of the world's nobility — by import. Then who is the Dormas upper class? Locked rooms, locked answer.

faction_state.tensions: Lady Oriane's tailor saw a thing he should not have seen. She has not yet decided whether he is loyal enough to live with the seeing.

name: Fluxom
motto: We made the trade. We will collect.
goal: Reopen the sea trade routes that collapsed under sea-creature attacks. Rebuild merchant dominance. Pretend the kingdoms condemned them for nothing.
fears: the sea routes never reopening
a third family rising and breaking the duopoly
Sahm's iron underbidding theirs
tension_hook: Fluxom condemned the kingdoms for actions taken at sea. The kingdoms condemned Fluxom for what it does to its own people. Both condemnations are correct.

faction_state.tensions: The two families are arguing over who gets the next sea-creature contract. The argument has cost three lives and a wing of the compound.

name: Nil
motto: We were here before the words.
goal: Hold the inner grove. Remember everything. Refuse to share most of it. Outlive whatever comes next.
fears: a successful incursion
Masond pushing harder for the other half of the memory
their own youth choosing to leave the grove
tension_hook: No army has ever taken Nil. The pure Tunyans carry a passive refusal-field that dampens hostile intent within their borders. They will not say what it is. The world has stopped asking.

faction_state.tensions: A young Tunyan left Nil for a season three years ago. He returned. He has not spoken since. The grove-mother has not asked him to.

name: Akeia
motto: Treaties are negotiated in the bedchamber. Wars are not.
goal: Maintain matriarchal monarchy as a serious diplomatic surface — marriage as state-merger, courtship as treaty negotiation, the queen's bedroom as council chamber. Treat curiosity from the rest of the world as the soft power it is.
fears: a foreign monarch mistaking the contract for entertainment
a queen-consort defecting with state secrets
a daughter refusing the throne
tension_hook: Curiosity, not condemnation, is the world's posture toward Akeia. Akeia knows. Curiosity is a position you can negotiate with.

faction_state.tensions: The queen mother's daughter has refused the formal succession. She has also refused to leave the palace. The chamberlain is running out of polite framings.
